methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Galadyne is a pre-seed stage defense and security company founded by Chandler Luzsicza that has raised $4.8M to date. The broader Defense & Security sector comprises 21 identifiable peers, including 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms such as Anduril Industries, Scale AI, and Shield AI. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within this narrow vertical to support a reliable median-multiple valuation approach.

Consequently, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable triangulation method. Using the $4.8M total raised as a proxy for post-money valuation, the analysis applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of pre-seed to seed-stage advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $12M. This represents standard progression dynamics for early-stage companies advancing beyond initial capital formation.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $10M to $14M, with a median point estimate of $12M. This single-method conclusion carries material execution and market risk; the absence of tight peer comparables in this stage and sector means the range reflects primarily early-stage mechanics rather than sector-specific benchmarking. Key uncertainties include future funding trajectory, customer traction, and technical validation relative to established defense-tech competitors.
